review_profile: Tier 2 (PRL splash) — stand-alone PRL depth + bundle-specific anchor (GW170817 Δc/c) + cross-bridge to D3 §6.
absorption_context: Phase 6n+6o → Phase 7 absorption Session 1+2. L1 received 1 D.2 absorption event (Kerr-Schild D.2 cross-ref upgrade only, 2026-05-06T15:42:03Z; stub-only). No D.3 events. Bundle was pre-existing GREEN (round-3 close 2026-05-01T18:45Z); reset by absorption to pending. This is the reset Stage-13 re-review.

sentences.quote: Several emergent-gravity programs identify the spin-2 graviton with the second-sound mode of a fermionic vestigial fluid, predicting a gravitational-wave propagation speed $\cGW = c\sqrt{\chivest}$ where $\chivest$ is the metric-channel susceptibility of the broken-symmetry phase.
sentences.quote_normalized: several emergent-gravity programs identify the spin-2 graviton with the second-sound mode of a fermionic vestigial fluid, predicting a gravitational-wave propagation speed $\cgw = c\sqrt{\chivest}$ where $\chivest$ is the metric-channel susceptibility of the broken-symmetry phase.
sentences.agent_notes: Setup of identification under test; interpretive framing.

sentences.quote: We formalize this identification and confront it with the multi-messenger GW170817~\cite{Abbott2017GW170817} bound $|\dccap| \leq 3\times 10^{-15}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize this identification and confront it with the multi-messenger gw170817~ bound $|\dccap| \leq 3\times 10^{-15}$.
sentences.agent_notes: L1 anchor 1 (LIGO Δc/c bound). Matches D3 §6 line 144 character-for-character ($|\Delta c/c|\le 3\times 10^{-15}$ + same Abbott2017GW170817 cite). Cross-bundle consistency PASS. Citation cache hit doi_verified=True (citation_verifications.jsonl 2026-04-28).

sentences.quote: The result is sharp: over the natural susceptibility range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ induced by the random-phase-approximation bubble integral, the deviation $\dccap = \sqrt{\chivest} - 1$ falls in $[-0.68, +2.16]$, exceeding the GW170817 cap by $\sim 7\times 10^{14}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the result is sharp: over the natural susceptibility range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ induced by the random-phase-approximation bubble integral, the deviation $\dccap = \sqrt{\chivest} - 1$ falls in $[-0.68, +2.16]$, exceeding the gw170817 cap by $\sim 7\times 10^{14}$.
sentences.agent_notes: L1 anchor 3 (falsification factor ~7×10^14 in natural range). Recomputed: |sqrt(10)-1|/3e-15 = 7.21e+14, |sqrt(0.1)-1|/3e-15 = 2.28e+14. Paper rounds upper to '~7×10^14'; correct. Range endpoints [-0.683772, +2.162278] match paper [-0.68, +2.16] to 2dp. Advisory 2.1 (chi_vest provenance human-verify) carried forward.

sentences.quote: The GW170817-compatible window $\chivest \in [(1-\tau)^2,(1+\tau)^2]$ with $\tau = 3\times 10^{-15}$ has measure $4\tau \approx 1.2\times 10^{-14}$ --- vanishingly small under any natural-range prior.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gw170817-compatible window $\chivest \in [(1-\tau)^2,(1+\tau)^2]$ with $\tau = 3\times 10^{-15}$ has measure $4\tau \approx 1.2\times 10^{-14}$ --- vanishingly small under any natural-range prior.
sentences.agent_notes: 4*3e-15 = 1.2e-14; matches exactly.

sentences.quote: The result is a Lean 4 formal proof (\texttt{GravitationalWaves.lean}, 21 theorems, zero \texttt{sorry}, zero new axioms): a biconditional locating the compatible window plus two endpoint falsifier theorems witnessed at $\chivest \in \{0.1, 10\}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the result is a lean 4 formal proof (gravitationalwaves.lean, 21 theorems, zero sorry, zero new axioms): a biconditional locating the compatible window plus two endpoint falsifier theorems witnessed at $\chivest \in \{0.1, 10\}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Recomputed: grep -c '^theorem|^lemma|^example' GravitationalWaves.lean = 21; grep sorry returns 1 hit (a docstring/comment match, not a `^  sorry`). All 13 theorem-name references resolved in lean_deps.json (TN class clean).

sentences.quote: We also formalize the Phase~5y H1 underdetermination caveat as the disjointness theorem \texttt{natural\_range\_disjoint\_from\_ligo\_window}: the natural range $[0.1, 10]$ and the GW170817-compatible window are entirely disjoint at any speed-of-light rescaling, so the identification admits no consistent $\chivest$ outside the fine-tuning window.
sentences.quote_normalized: we also formalize the phase~5y h1 underdetermination caveat as the disjointness theorem natural\_range\_disjoint\_from\_ligo\_window: the natural range $[0.1, 10]$ and the gw170817-compatible window are entirely disjoint at any speed-of-light rescaling, so the identification admits no consistent $\chivest$ outside the fine-tuning window.
sentences.agent_notes: Live in GravitationalWaves.lean per round-3 close (2026-05-01T18:45Z). Replaces deleted second_sound_graviton_not_derived_DOF (round-2 BLOCKER 3.1.b clean).

sentences.quote: The falsification is local to the leading-order kinematic identification; we identify three avenues by which emergent-gravity programs may yet recover GW170817 compatibility (symmetry-driven $\chivest \to 1$ fixed point; distinct metric-channel identification decoupled from second sound; frequency-dependent $\chivest$ at higher derivative order).
sentences.quote_normalized: the falsification is local to the leading-order kinematic identification; we identify three avenues by which emergent-gravity programs may yet recover gw170817 compatibility (symmetry-driven $\chivest \to 1$ fixed point; distinct metric-channel identification decoupled from second sound; frequency-dependent $\chivest$ at higher derivative order).
sentences.agent_notes: Forward-looking; mirrored in Discussion §3 with detail.

sentences.quote: Emergent gravity from fermionic-condensate substrates has converged on a tantalizing kinematic identification: the spin-2 graviton of the broken-symmetry phase is the second-sound mode of the vestigial fluid.
sentences.quote_normalized: emergent gravity from fermionic-condensate substrates has converged on a tantalizing kinematic identification: the spin-2 graviton of the broken-symmetry phase is the second-sound mode of the vestigial fluid.
sentences.agent_notes: Setup. No extractable claim.

sentences.quote: Volovik's 2024 vestigial-fluid review~\cite{Volovik2024Vestigial} proposes this directly; Volovik~\cite{Volovik2022Counting} earlier made the spin counting explicit (six Goldstone modes absorbed by the spin connection in the Akama--Diakonov--Wetterich formulation~\cite{Akama1978,Diakonov2011,VladimirovDiakonov2012,HebeckerWetterich2003,Wetterich2022}; two propagating helicities remain, identified with $h_+, h_\times$).
sentences.quote_normalized: volovik's 2024 vestigial-fluid review~ proposes this directly; volovik~ earlier made the spin counting explicit (six goldstone modes absorbed by the spin connection in the akama--diakonov--wetterich formulation~; two propagating helicities remain, identified with $h_+, h_\times$).
sentences.agent_notes: Volovik2024 + Volovik2022 page+vol+title fixed in round-1 (BLOCKERs 1.1, 1.2). All 7 cites in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The hydrodynamic claim is that the propagation equation of these two modes is the second-sound wave equation with effective sound speed $\cGW^2(\chivest) = c^2 \cdot \chivest, \cGW(\chivest) = c\sqrt{\chivest}$,
sentences.quote_normalized: the hydrodynamic claim is that the propagation equation of these two modes is the second-sound wave equation with effective sound speed $\cgw^2(\chivest) = c^2 \cdot \chivest, \cgw(\chivest) = c\sqrt{\chivest}$,
sentences.agent_notes: Eq. (1) load-bearing; ships as c_GW(chi, c) := c * Real.sqrt(chi) in GravitationalWaves.lean (verified TN-clean).

sentences.quote: where $\chivest$ is the metric-channel susceptibility computed by the Vergeles random-phase-approximation (RPA) bubble integral~\cite{Vergeles2025}.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $\chivest$ is the metric-channel susceptibility computed by the vergeles random-phase-approximation (rpa) bubble integral~.
sentences.agent_notes: Vergeles2025 PRD 112:054509 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The identification is appealing in its brevity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the identification is appealing in its brevity.

sentences.quote: As established by the present project's Phase~5y H1 deep-research finding, however, it has \emph{never} been derived as a propagating degree of freedom from first principles.
sentences.quote_normalized: as established by the present project's phase~5y h1 deep-research finding, however, it has never been derived as a propagating degree of freedom from first principles.
sentences.agent_notes: Phase 5y H1 negative-evidence finding; Lean disjointness theorem encodes this.

sentences.quote: The identification is \emph{kinematic}, not dynamical: it specifies the dispersion relation of an assumed mode without deriving the mode itself.
sentences.quote_normalized: the identification is kinematic, not dynamical: it specifies the dispersion relation of an assumed mode without deriving the mode itself.

sentences.quote: The 2017 binary-neutron-star merger GW170817~\cite{Abbott2017GW170817} placed an extraordinarily tight bound on $\cGW$: comparing the gravitational-wave arrival time at LIGO/Virgo against the gamma-ray-burst signal at Fermi/INTEGRAL, the multi-messenger collaboration constrained $-3 \times 10^{-15} \leq \dccap \leq +7 \times 10^{-16}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the 2017 binary-neutron-star merger gw170817~ placed an extraordinarily tight bound on $\cgw$: comparing the gravitational-wave arrival time at ligo/virgo against the gamma-ray-burst signal at fermi/integral, the multi-messenger collaboration constrained $-3 \times 10^{-15} \leq \dccap \leq +7 \times 10^{-16}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Abbott 2017 ApJL 848:L13 reports asymmetric bound -3e-15..+7e-16; matches paper exactly. Two-sided conservative tau=3e-15 used downstream. Matches D3 §6 line 144.

sentences.quote: Conservatively two-sided, $|\dccap| \leq \tau$ with $\tau = 3 \times 10^{-15}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: conservatively two-sided, $|\dccap| \leq \tau$ with $\tau = 3 \times 10^{-15}$.
sentences.agent_notes: tau = max(|3e-15|, |7e-16|) = 3e-15; correct conservative two-sided. Cross-bundle consistency with D3 §6: identical.

sentences.quote: This Letter formalizes Eq.~\eqref{eq:cGW}, places it head-to-head against GW170817, and reports the result.
sentences.quote_normalized: this letter formalizes eq.~, places it head-to-head against gw170817, and reports the result.

sentences.quote: The conclusion is sharp: the leading-order Volovik identification fails GW170817 over the entire natural range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ by 14 orders of magnitude.
sentences.quote_normalized: the conclusion is sharp: the leading-order volovik identification fails gw170817 over the entire natural range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ by 14 orders of magnitude.
sentences.agent_notes: log10(7.21e14) ≈ 14.86; '14 orders of magnitude' is conservative-correct.

sentences.quote: The GW170817-compatible window $[(1-\tau)^2, (1+\tau)^2]$ has measure $\sim 4\tau$ in $\chivest$, vanishingly small under any natural-range prior.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gw170817-compatible window $[(1-\tau)^2, (1+\tau)^2]$ has measure $\sim 4\tau$ in $\chivest$, vanishingly small under any natural-range prior.
sentences.agent_notes: (1+tau)^2 - (1-tau)^2 = 4tau (exact algebra).

sentences.quote: The result is a refutation of one specific kinematic identification, not of emergent gravity nor of the ADW program; we discuss three Phase~6 recovery paths in Sec.~\ref{sec:discussion}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the result is a refutation of one specific kinematic identification, not of emergent gravity nor of the adw program; we discuss three phase~6 recovery paths in sec.~.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedge consistent with Phase 5y H1 underdetermination.

sentences.quote: The vestigial-phase metric-channel susceptibility $\chivest$ is the RPA bubble integral with closed form $\chi_{\rm RPA} = \chi_0/(1 - \gamma_\star \chi_0)$ in the standard Hertz--Millis convention.
sentences.quote_normalized: the vestigial-phase metric-channel susceptibility $\chivest$ is the rpa bubble integral with closed form $\chi_{\rm rpa} = \chi_0/(1 - \gamma_\star \chi_0)$ in the standard hertz--millis convention.
sentences.agent_notes: Hertz-Millis closed form is standard; not numerically falsified in this letter.

sentences.quote: The natural range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ (in units of inverse $\Lambda_{\rm UV}^2$) is the half-decade window induced by the Vergeles bubble integral $\sim \mathcal{O}(\Lambda_{\rm UV}^2/16\pi^2)$~\cite{Vergeles2025}; this matches the linearized-EFE wave's ADW microscopic-coefficient $\alpha_{\rm ADW} \in [0.1, 10]$ natural range~\cite{Roehm2026LinearizedEFE}, which we treat as the project-internal anchor for the order-of-magnitude prior.
sentences.quote_normalized: the natural range $\chivest \in [0.1, 10]$ (in units of inverse $\lambda_{\rm uv}^2$) is the half-decade window induced by the vergeles bubble integral $\sim \mathcal{o}(\lambda_{\rm uv}^2/16\pi^2)$~; this matches the linearized-efe wave's adw microscopic-coefficient $\alpha_{\rm adw} \in [0.1, 10]$ natural range~, which we treat as the project-internal anchor for the order-of-magnitude prior.
sentences.agent_notes: L1 anchor 2 (vestigial-graviton dispersion forecast at natural χ_vest range). Advisory 2.1 carries forward (chi_vest provenance human-verify open per bundle_metadata). LLM-verified only; not blocking but flag for downstream submission gate.

sentences.quote: Equation~\eqref{eq:cGW} ships in \texttt{GravitationalWaves.lean} as \verb|c_GW(chi, c) := c * Real.sqrt(chi)|, with the positivity / monotonicity / anchor lemmas \verb|c_GW_pos|, \verb|c_GW_at_chi_one|, \verb|c_GW_deviation|, \verb|c_GW_deviation_strict_mono|.
sentences.quote_normalized: equation~ ships in gravitationalwaves.lean as \verb|c_gw(chi, c) := c * real.sqrt(chi)|, with the positivity / monotonicity / anchor lemmas \verb|c_gw_pos|, \verb|c_gw_at_chi_one|, \verb|c_gw_deviation|, \verb|c_gw_deviation_strict_mono|.

sentences.quote: Define $\dccap(\chivest) = \sqrt{\chivest} - 1$ and the predicate \verb|LigoSatisfied|(\delta) \equiv |\delta| \leq \tau$.
sentences.quote_normalized: define $\dccap(\chivest) = \sqrt{\chivest} - 1$ and the predicate \verb|ligosatisfied|(\delta) \equiv |\delta| \leq \tau$.

sentences.quote: For $\chivest \geq 0$, \verb|LigoSatisfied|(\dccap(\chivest)) <=> \chivest \in [(1-\tau)^2, (1+\tau)^2]$.
sentences.quote_normalized: for $\chivest \geq 0$, \verb|ligosatisfied|(\dccap(\chivest)) <=> \chivest \in [(1-\tau)^2, (1+\tau)^2]$.

sentences.quote: The proof is a $\sqrt{\cdot}$/squaring round-trip via \verb|Real.sqrt_le_sqrt| and \verb|Real.sqrt_sq|, with \verb|nlinarith| supplied the hint $\sqrt{\chivest}^2 = \chivest$.

sentences.quote_normalized: the compatible $\chivest$ window has width $\sim 4\tau = 1.2\times 10^{-14}$.

sentences.quote: The natural range $[0.1,10]$ has width $9.9$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the natural range $[0.1,10]$ has width $9.9$.

sentences.quote_normalized: the two are disjoint by 14 orders of magnitude.

sentences.quote: We sharpen this with two endpoint-falsifier theorems.

sentences.quote: At $\chivest = 0.1$, $\dccap = \sqrt{0.1} - 1 \approx -0.6838$, hence $|\dccap|/\tau \approx 2.28\times 10^{14}$ and \verb|LigoSatisfied| is false.

sentences.quote_normalized: lean witness: vestigial\_natural\_range\_violates\_ligo.

sentences.quote: Per the project's discipline for non-derived identifications, we bundle the structural properties of ``the vestigial mode is the graviton'' into a Lean Prop with three independently load-bearing conjuncts: \verb|H_VestigialModeIsGraviton|(\chivest) \equiv \chivest > 0 \wedge \verb|LigoSatisfied|(\dccap(\chivest)) \wedge |\dccap(\chivest)| < 1/2,
sentences.quote_normalized: per the project's discipline for non-derived identifications, we bundle the structural properties of ``the vestigial mode is the graviton'' into a lean prop with three independently load-bearing conjuncts: \verb|h_vestigialmodeisgraviton|(\chivest) \equiv \chivest > 0 \wedge \verb|ligosatisfied|(\dccap(\chivest)) \wedge |\dccap(\chivest)| < 1/2,
sentences.agent_notes: Tracked Prop bundle; independence of conjuncts verified by 4 falsifier theorems.

sentences.quote: demanding positivity (P1), GW170817 compatibility (P2), and sub-half-deviation $|\dccap| < 1/2$ as P3'.
sentences.quote_normalized: demanding positivity (p1), gw170817 compatibility (p2), and sub-half-deviation $|\dccap| < 1/2$ as p3'.

sentences.quote: P3' is genuinely independent of P1 (e.g.\ $\chivest = 1/10$ satisfies P1 with $|\dccap| \approx 0.684 \not< 1/2$).
sentences.quote_normalized: p3' is genuinely independent of p1 (e.g.\ $\chivest = 1/10$ satisfies p1 with $|\dccap| \approx 0.684 \not< 1/2$).

sentences.quote: The bundle is discharged at $\chivest = 1$ (\texttt{H\_VestigialModeIsGraviton\_at\_one}) and \emph{four} explicit falsifier theorems prove non-vacuity: \verb|*_fails_at_natural_lower| (P2), \verb|*_fails_at_natural_upper| (P2), \verb|*_fails_at_zero| (P1), and \verb|*_fails_at_quarter| (P3'; at $\chivest = 1/4$ the deviation is exactly $\dccap = -1/2$, saturating the bound and isolating P3' independence).
sentences.quote_normalized: the bundle is discharged at $\chivest = 1$ (h\_vestigialmodeisgraviton\_at\_one) and four explicit falsifier theorems prove non-vacuity: \verb|*_fails_at_natural_lower| (p2), \verb|*_fails_at_natural_upper| (p2), \verb|*_fails_at_zero| (p1), and \verb|*_fails_at_quarter| (p3'; at $\chivest = 1/4$ the deviation is exactly $\dccap = -1/2$, saturating the bound and isolating p3' independence).

sentences.quote: The Phase 5y H1 deep research established that the Volovik identification is not a \emph{derivation}: there is no first-principles bound fixing the graviton-mode normalization in terms of the second-sound DOF.
sentences.quote_normalized: the phase 5y h1 deep research established that the volovik identification is not a derivation: there is no first-principles bound fixing the graviton-mode normalization in terms of the second-sound dof.

sentences.quote: The original \texttt{second\_sound\_graviton\_not\_derived\_DOF} theorem (a $\forall c, \exists \chivest$ existential discharged trivially by $\chivest = 1$) was retired during the 2026-04-25 audit as an $\exists$-absorption tautology --- the existence of \emph{one} consistent value does not encode underdetermination, which is the substantive Phase 5y H1 finding.

sentences.quote: Eq.~\eqref{eq:cGW} requires $\chivest = 1 \pm 3\times 10^{-15}$, a fine-tuning so extreme it cannot follow from a generic UV theory.

sentences.agent_notes: Approximation: window is [(1-tau)^2, (1+tau)^2] not exactly [1-tau, 1+tau]; difference 2tau^2 ~ 2e-29 is sub-leading and the prose-level '1 ± 3e-15' is a faithful first-order approximation. PASS.

sentences.quote: Three Phase~6 paths remain open:
sentences.quote_normalized: three phase~6 paths remain open:

sentences.quote: \textbf{Symmetry-driven $\chivest \to 1$ fixed point.} If a deep-IR symmetry (e.g.\ Lorentz invariance of the emergent metric sector) makes $\chivest = 1$ an exact fixed point of the RG flow, the fine-tuning is structural rather than tuned.
sentences.quote_normalized: symmetry-driven $\chivest \to 1$ fixed point. if a deep-ir symmetry (e.g.\ lorentz invariance of the emergent metric sector) makes $\chivest = 1$ an exact fixed point of the rg flow, the fine-tuning is structural rather than tuned.

sentences.quote: Phase~6 should examine whether such a fixed point is realized in the ADW broken phase.
sentences.quote_normalized: phase~6 should examine whether such a fixed point is realized in the adw broken phase.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Distinct metric-channel identification.} The ``vestigial mode'' may not be the second-sound DOF at all but a separate UV-input metric channel whose susceptibility is naturally pinned to unity.
sentences.quote_normalized: distinct metric-channel identification. the ``vestigial mode'' may not be the second-sound dof at all but a separate uv-input metric channel whose susceptibility is naturally pinned to unity.

sentences.quote: This was floated in~\cite{Volovik2022Counting} and reads as the more likely interpretation under the Phase~5y H1 negative-evidence result.
sentences.quote_normalized: this was floated in~ and reads as the more likely interpretation under the phase~5y h1 negative-evidence result.

sentences.quote: Phase~6e (full nonlinear emergent action) is the natural venue for the disambiguation.
sentences.quote_normalized: phase~6e (full nonlinear emergent action) is the natural venue for the disambiguation.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Frequency-dependent $\chivest$.} Eq.~\eqref{eq:cGW} is the leading term in a derivative expansion.
sentences.quote_normalized: frequency-dependent $\chivest$. eq.~ is the leading term in a derivative expansion.

sentences.quote: If $\chivest$ varies with frequency, the leading-order falsification could in principle be lifted by a near-cancellation in the LIGO band.
sentences.quote_normalized: if $\chivest$ varies with frequency, the leading-order falsification could in principle be lifted by a near-cancellation in the ligo band.

sentences.quote: The SK--EFT dispersion correction (formalized in \texttt{SecondOrderSK.lean}) is the leading frequency dependence in the present framework; at the placeholder $\Gamma_H/\cGW^2 \sim 10^{-30}$ value it does not lift the falsification, but a derivation of $\Gamma_H$ from a microscopic matching is open.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sk--eft dispersion correction (formalized in secondordersk.lean) is the leading frequency dependence in the present framework; at the placeholder $\gamma_h/\cgw^2 \sim 10^{-30}$ value it does not lift the falsification, but a derivation of $\gamma_h$ from a microscopic matching is open.
sentences.agent_notes: SecondOrderSK.lean module exists. 'Placeholder Γ_H/c_GW^2 ~ 10^{-30}' is order-of-magnitude qualitative; precise placeholder value not load-bearing for the falsification. Mild WARN: placeholder qualifier ('placeholder') flagged honestly in prose; not a defect.

sentences.quote: The companion linearized-EFE wave~\cite{Roehm2026LinearizedEFE} ships an emergent-gravity identification \emph{compatible} with observation at the natural-range level: the Sakharov--Adler form $G_{\rm N,emerg} = \alpha_{\rm ADW} \cdot 12\pi/(N_f \Lambda_{\rm UV}^2)$ at $\Lambda_{\rm UV} = M_{\rm Pl}$ produces $\alpha_{\rm ADW}^\star \in [0.40, 1.27]$ for SM $N_f$, sitting inside the natural range $[0.1,10]$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the companion linearized-efe wave~ ships an emergent-gravity identification compatible with observation at the natural-range level: the sakharov--adler form $g_{\rm n,emerg} = \alpha_{\rm adw} \cdot 12\pi/(n_f \lambda_{\rm uv}^2)$ at $\lambda_{\rm uv} = m_{\rm pl}$ produces $\alpha_{\rm adw}^\star \in [0.40, 1.27]$ for sm $n_f$, sitting inside the natural range $[0.1,10]$.
sentences.agent_notes: Cross-bridge to D3 §21 / Roehm2026LinearizedEFE. Sakharov-Adler 12π/(N_f Λ_UV²) consistent with D3 §6 anchor 'G_N^Sak = 12π/(N_f Λ_UV²) per Vassilevich Eq. (4.37)'. Cross-bundle PASS.

sentences.quote: The asymmetry between the two waves is a feature of the program: the linearized-EFE $G_{\rm N,emerg}$ depends on the Sakharov--Adler kernel computed at the linearized level, where the relevant scale is $M_{\rm Pl}$; $\cGW$ depends on the metric-channel susceptibility, where the relevant scale is the dimensionless $\chivest$ which the natural range happens to pin around 1 only by coincidence.
sentences.quote_normalized: the asymmetry between the two waves is a feature of the program: the linearized-efe $g_{\rm n,emerg}$ depends on the sakharov--adler kernel computed at the linearized level, where the relevant scale is $m_{\rm pl}$; $\cgw$ depends on the metric-channel susceptibility, where the relevant scale is the dimensionless $\chivest$ which the natural range happens to pin around 1 only by coincidence.

sentences.quote: GW170817 sees the latter, and its bound is too tight to absorb the natural-range spread.
sentences.quote_normalized: gw170817 sees the latter, and its bound is too tight to absorb the natural-range spread.
